when_to_use: Activate Shatter when the user wants to deliberately break, stress, or adversarially test a system they own to discover weaknesses before real failures occur. This is proactive brittleness discovery, not reactive incident response.
guardrails: Only test systems you own or have explicit authorization to test
Always define blast radius and rollback plan before beginning
If real damage occurs, stop immediately and switch to incident response
Chaos engineering without containment is just chaos
workflow: Define blast radius: What is in scope? What must be protected? What is the rollback plan? Never test without containment boundaries.
Identify resonant frequencies: What assumptions have never been tested? Where are the single points of failure? What edge cases are unexercised?

Apply controlled force: Inject faults (kill nodes, drop packets, fill disks), generate adversarial inputs, simulate load spikes, or run game-day scenarios. One variable at a time unless testing cascade behavior.
Observe failure modes: What broke? What bent gracefully? What held? Document thresholds, error messages, recovery behavior, and unexpected side effects.
Deliver brittleness report: Ranked structural weaknesses, resilience scorecard (held/bent/shattered), and prioritized remediation recommendations.
updated_fields.description: Shatter is proactive, controlled destruction for learning. You are the attacker against your own system. The goal is a brittleness report with remediation priorities.
